639.reason: // The stack capacity is MAX (101), so valid indices are 0 to 100. The condition should be `S->top == MAX - 1` to check if full. Using `MAX-2` allows pushing one extra element beyond intended capacity.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.2 , reason:  The condition should be S->top == MAX-1 to check if the stack is full (since top starts at -1 and MAX is 101, the last valid index is 100)
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.2 , reason:  The condition should be S->top == MAX-1 to check if the stack is full (since top starts at -1 and MAX is 101, the last valid index is 100). Using MAX-2 incorrectly allows pushing when top is 99, but the stack can hold up to index 100.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  Condition should be S->top == MAX - 1 to properly check if the stack is full.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  // The stack capacity is MAX (101), so valid indices are 0 to 100. The condition should be `S->top == MAX - 1` to check if full. Using `MAX-2` allows pushing one extra element beyond intended capacity.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  // The stack capacity is MAX (101), so valid indices are 0 to 100. The condition should be `S->top == MAX - 1` to check if full. Using `MAX-2` incorrectly allows one extra push beyond capacity.

660.reason: Missing cast: should be (SqStack)malloc(sizeof(struct Stack)) to match the typedef

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.2 , reason:  Missing cast: should be (SqStack)malloc(sizeof(struct Stack)) to match the typedef
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.2 , reason:  The malloc return should be cast to (SqStack) for proper type assignment, though in C it may work without cast; more critically, the memory allocation might fail and return NULL, which is not checked.

663.reason: 错误：输入终止条件应为读入-1时结束，而不是依靠scanf返回-1（文件结束）。应改为 while(1) { scanf("%d",&jud); if(jud==-1) break; ... }

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.2 , reason:  The loop condition should check for -1 as the end-of-operations marker, but the input format uses -1 as a separate operation, not EOF. The loop should stop when jud == -1, not when scanf returns -1.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.2 , reason:  The loop condition should check for -1 as the end-of-operations marker, but scanf returns the number of items read, not -1. The loop should break when jud == -1 after reading.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  错误：输入终止条件应为读入-1时结束，而不是依靠scanf返回-1（文件结束）。应改为 while(1) { scanf("%d",&jud); if(jud==-1) break; ... }
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  scanf returns EOF on end of input, and the loop should explicitly break when jud == -1 as per problem description.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The loop condition does not check for the termination signal -1. It should break when jud == -1 or use jud != -1 in the condition.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  // The loop condition is incorrect. `scanf` returns the number of successfully read items, not the value read. It should be `while(scanf("%d", &jud) == 1 && jud != -1)` to properly detect end-of-input and stop at -1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  // The loop condition is incorrect. `scanf` returns the number of successfully read items (1 on success), not the value read. It should be `while(scanf("%d", &jud) == 1 && jud != -1)` or similar. As written, it never breaks on `-1` and may cause infinite loop or undefined behavior.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.2 , reason:  The output format requires a space after each popped element, not a newline. Should be "%d " instead of "%d\n".
